userNote: STRUCTURAL DEBT: the onFileChange callback at lines 207-419 is the ~215-line inline orchestration that HOOK-ARCHITECTURE-RESEARCH §6 explicitly flagged as the biggest pre-hook procedural seam left in the codebase. HOOKS.FILE_BEFORE_CHANGE and HOOKS.FILE_AFTER_CHANGE are declared in hook-registry.js with documented payloads but have zero publishers. This is what those hooks were defined for. Splitting the add/change/unlink branches into FILE_BEFORE_CHANGE + FILE_AFTER_CHANGE subscribers is the natural next batch — it unlocks per-file plugins (Louis lock checks, real-time UI mirrors, audit listeners) without patching main.js for every new concern.
actionsTaken: Resolved upstream by Wave 2B — see hooks ticket 6. The new onFileChange wrapper is 69 lines (40 LOC of real code) with two FILE_AFTER_CHANGE subscribers (schema-card-emitter P=20, sse-broadcaster P=30). Confirmed via `git log --oneline --grep='decompose 215-line'` → commit 13ec011 'refactor(hooks): decompose 215-line onFileChange into FILE_BEFORE/AFTER hooks'. The onFileChange callback in src/core/server/main.js now fires HOOKS.FILE_BEFORE_CHANGE (line 411) and HOOKS.FILE_AFTER_CHANGE (line 431), with the add/change/unlink dispatch reduced to a thin orchestrator. The named hook constants now have real publishers and Louis lock checks / real-time UI mirrors / audit listeners can subscribe without patching main.js. No Wave 4A code change required.
reviewNote: Verified. `git log --grep='decompose 215-line'` returns commit 13ec011; `git show 13ec011` confirms _applyFileChange helper + FILE_BEFORE_CHANGE / FILE_AFTER_CHANGE publishers with two subscribers (schema-card-emitter P=20, sse-broadcaster P=30) registered upstream. 4A commit 1bf216f is JSON-only (1 file, 7 insertions) — correct posture for resolved-upstream.

userNote: REDUNDANT POLL: setInterval at line 199 hits /api/connection-state.json every 5s purely to recolor constellation particles. The same data (mutation events → status changes) already flows through the SSE stream this same file subscribes to at line 903. Subscribe the constellation update to the existing mutationSource.onmessage handler instead and drop the poll. Comment at line 197 even calls this out: 'piggyback by listening on the same window event the toast handler emits, but to keep coupling loose we just poll the manifest periodically too (cheap; 5s).' Cheap, but redundant — and a 5s ceiling on latency for status changes that SSE delivers in <1s.

REDUNDANT POLL: startPolling polls /api/connection-state.json every 2s via setInterval at line 65. The notification bus already exposes the same mutation data over SSE at /api/mutations, AND no current consumer of St8Coordination.addListener exists in src/frontend/ (grep confirms no addListener call sites). Either wire coordination to the SSE stream (cheap, real-time) or delete the service if nothing actually subscribes. Today it does work every 2s that no one reads.

NO TESTS: the debounce-then-flush behavior (_onFileChange + _flush at lines 93-120) is the core of the watcher and has zero unit tests. Edge cases that need coverage: (a) rapid CREATE+EDIT for the same file inside one debounce window — what arrives in the flush array? (b) DELETE arriving during an in-flight flush — does the next debounce see it? (c) callback rejection — does the watcher recover for the next event? (d) pending-changes Set semantics: { path, type } objects are reference-equal not value-equal, so duplicates accumulate. Hand-test today, regression-prone forever without coverage.
actionsTaken: Added tests/features/watcher/file-watcher.test.js with 11 real probes of the in-process state machine (chokidar never started — probes drive _onFileChange / _flush / stop directly with debounceMs=30). Coverage: (1) 25 same-(path,type) events collapse to 1 flush entry — proves ticket-4 dedup, (2) distinct paths all flush in one batch, (3) CREATE+EDIT on same path stays as TWO entries — guards against the trap of Map<path,type> last-write-wins which would silently drop main.js's add-branch dispatch, (4) flush handler receives values Array (not Map), (5) timer resets on each event (debounce, not throttle) — drips events every DEBOUNCE-10 ms for three windows and asserts exactly one flush at the end, (6) stop() cancels pending timer and prevents flush — also asserts debounceTimer === null post-stop, (7) onFileChange rejection — watcher still flushes the next event (callCount==2), (8) metrics.eventsReceived counts every input event, (9) metrics.debounceMergeCount counts only duplicates, (10) metrics.flushCalls + lastFlushAt + lastFlushSize update across two distinct flush windows; lastFlushAt is Date.parse-able, (11) getMetrics() returns an isolated snapshot — mutating the returned object does not bleed back. Suite size: 207 → 218 (+11). Full `npm test` clean.
reviewNote: Verified 11 real probes drive the actual _onFileChange/_flush/stop with real setTimeout (debounceMs=30, no fake clocks, no SUT mocks, no assert.ok(true) cheats). Targeted run: 11 pass / 0 fail in 1217 ms. Probes cover dedup, CREATE+EDIT preservation, Array-not-Map handler arg, debounce-reset (drip every DEBOUNCE-10 ms × 3 = exactly 1 flush), stop() timer cancellation, handler-rejection recovery, and all 5 metrics counters including snapshot isolation.

userNote: BUG-RISK: pendingChanges is `new Set()` but entries are pushed as `{ path, type }` object literals (line 94). Each call creates a fresh object so the Set never deduplicates — a file with 100 'change' events in one debounce window produces 100 entries in the flush. Either switch to a `Map` keyed by `path` (last write wins, deduped) or a `Map<path, Set<type>>` if all event types matter. Today it just inflates the flush array.
actionsTaken: Switched pendingChanges from `new Set()` to `new Map()` keyed by the composite string `${filePath}::${eventType}`. Chose Map+composite-key over Map<path,type> (last-write-wins) because the existing `onFileChange` callback in main.js dispatches by `change.type` — collapsing a CREATE+EDIT for the same path to one EDIT would silently drop the CREATE branch's persistence.upsertFile + initial schema-card emission. The composite key dedupes the storm-pattern (100 'change' events on one file → 1 entry) while preserving CREATE/EDIT/DELETE distinction within a window. Updated _onFileChange to set the key and _flush to iterate `pendingChanges.values()`. Smoke probe: 3 'change' events + 1 'add' on different paths → pendingChanges.size === 2, flush array has 2 entries. Test coverage lands in ticket 3.
reviewNote: Verified file-watcher.js:50 is `new Map()`; _onFileChange:149 builds composite key `${filePath}::${eventType}` and set()s the change object. Mutation probe: replacing the key with Symbol() flipped the dedup test (25→25 entries instead of 1) AND the merge-count test (0 instead of 3) — exactly the right two failures. CREATE+EDIT preservation probe confirms composite key avoids the Map<path>-last-write-wins trap so main.js's per-type dispatch remains correct.

userNote: SCHEMA GAP: file_registry.lifecyclePhase is TEXT DEFAULT 'DEVELOPMENT' with no CHECK constraint (line 60). Same for brunoStatus TEXT DEFAULT 'active' (line 70). A typo in _handleProductionPromote ('PORDUCTION') or any future setter lands silently. Add `CHECK (lifecyclePhase IN ('DEVELOPMENT', 'STAGING', 'PRODUCTION'))` and `CHECK (brunoStatus IN ('active', 'flagged', 'archived'))`. Indexes on both columns already exist (idx_file_registry_lifecycle, idx_file_registry_bruno) so the values are already grouped — the constraint just enforces what the queries already assume.
actionsTaken: Added CHECK constraints to ST8_SCHEMA in src/core/database/persistence.js: (1) lifecyclePhase CHECK IN ('CONCEPT','LOCKED','WIRING','DEVELOPMENT','PRODUCTION') — matches the canonical LifecyclePhase enum in src/shared/types/st8-types.js. The ticket's suggested ('DEVELOPMENT','STAGING','PRODUCTION') would have rejected the existing CONCEPT/LOCKED writes (_handleConceptFile lifecyclePhase='CONCEPT', _handleMvpLock UPDATE … SET lifecyclePhase='LOCKED'); the executed set is the actual production-used surface. STAGING is doc-only (roadmap aspirational) and is correctly excluded. (2) brunoStatus CHECK IN ('active','flagged','archived') — matches the runBrunoCall / archiveFile / onEventTriggered call sites verbatim. SQLite ALTER TABLE cannot add CHECK to an existing column — these constraints land only on NEW databases. Existing st8.sqlite files retain the un-constrained columns until a migration framework lands (correctly deferred to P1.1 persistence roadmap). JS-side validators (claimTicket provider check, logActivity throw-on-unknown) cover the existing-DB gap. Test: new tests/core/database/lifecycle-check-constraints.test.js with 5 probes — (a) every canonical lifecyclePhase enum value inserts cleanly, (b) typos PORDUCTION / staging (lowercase) / development (lowercase) / STAGING / unknown are rejected with 'CHECK constraint failed', (c) every brunoStatus enum value inserts cleanly, (d) typos ACTIVE / Active / archive / flag / unknown are rejected, (e) defaults still land — bare INSERT without lifecyclePhase/brunoStatus yields ('DEVELOPMENT','active'). Targeted run 5 pass; full suite 218→223.
residualConcerns: Existing st8.sqlite files (already-bootstrapped DBs) do not gain the CHECK constraint — SQLite cannot add CHECK via ALTER TABLE. A typo against a pre-existing DB would still land silently until a migration framework runs CREATE TABLE … new + INSERT SELECT + DROP + RENAME. The migration framework is the P1.1 persistence roadmap item — out of scope for Wave 4B.
reviewNote: Verified ST8_SCHEMA file_registry contains CHECK (lifecyclePhase IN ('CONCEPT','LOCKED','WIRING','DEVELOPMENT','PRODUCTION')) at persistence.js:41 and CHECK (brunoStatus IN ('active','flagged','archived')) at line 51. Executor's enum-correction call (CONCEPT/LOCKED retained vs ticket's suggested DEVELOPMENT/STAGING/PRODUCTION) is right — app.js:1031 writes CONCEPT and app.js:1086 writes LOCKED. Targeted suite 5/5 pass. Existing-DB residual correctly scoped to persistence-migration roadmap.

userNote: DOUBLE-FIRE INVESTIGATION: when the watcher fires CREATE then EDIT for the same path inside one debounce window, main.js's loop calls publish() twice. Both publishes (a) emit on the EventEmitter (b) broadcast SSE (c) console.log (d) if schemaCard present, call printer.printCard. The printer chain has no idempotency guard — a card with the same fingerprint could be written twice in <1s. Today the schemaCard field is unset by main.js publishers so the printer doesn't actually fire, but the moment any publisher attaches a card the double-fire becomes real. Either dedupe at the watcher (Map by path — see ticket above) or guard the printer write with a recent-fingerprint cache.

VERIFY SSE CLEANUP: addSSEClient at line 96 wires res.on('close') and res.on('error') to delete the response from this.sseClients. Confirmed via read: 'error' handler exists (added explicitly per the inline comment to prevent uncaught process crashes) and 'close' handler exists. One gap: there is no idle-timeout / heartbeat, so a half-open TCP that never fires 'close' or 'error' (e.g. a client whose machine sleeps) will sit in the Set indefinitely, capped at maxSseClients=10. Add a periodic ping (e.g. res.write(': heartbeat\n\n') every 30s) that fails the broken-pipe write loop and naturally drops dead clients via the per-client try/catch in _broadcastSSE.
actionsTaken: Audited addSSEClient — 'close' AND 'error' handlers both delete from this.sseClients; the 'error' handler also calls res.end() in a try/catch. The per-client try/catch in _broadcastSSE provides a third cleanup path (broken-pipe writes delete the client). All three paths are now covered by real integration probes. Added tests/core/notification-bus.test.js with 3 real-HTTP cleanup probes that boot a localhost http.Server piping every request through bus.addSSEClient (no SUT mock): (1) connect via http.request, assert sseClients.size becomes 1, call client.req.destroy() to trigger res.on('close'), poll until sseClients.size returns to 0 — proves the close-path cleanup, (2) connect, kill the underlying TCP socket (client.req.socket.destroy()), then call bus.publish — _broadcastSSE's per-client try/catch deletes the broken client when the write throws, proves the broadcast-error cleanup path, (3) open 3 clients, destroy the middle one, assert size==2 (only middle dropped), then destroy the other two, assert size==0 — proves independent per-client cleanup with no cross-contamination. The heartbeat half-open-TCP gap noted in the ticket is addressed by ticket 8 (this ticket's audit confirms the existing close/error/broadcast paths work as documented).
residualConcerns: Half-open TCP (machine-sleep scenario) with neither 'close' nor 'error' fired before a publish will sit in the Set until maxSseClients=10 is hit. The heartbeat in ticket 8 closes this gap by forcing a periodic write that fails the broken-pipe path.

userNote: SSE ROBUSTNESS: /api/mutations endpoint (_handleMutationsSSE at line 755) is a one-liner — it just calls notificationBus.addSSEClient. There is no server-side heartbeat (see notification-bus ticket above), no max-age, and the only test for it is hand-curl. The frontend has reconnect-with-backoff (src/frontend/app.js:945), which masks but does not eliminate dropped connections. Add: (a) a server-side ping every 30s, (b) an integration test that connects, asserts handshake, holds the connection while publish() fires, and verifies the event arrives, (c) a reconnection test that drops mid-stream and verifies frontend recovers.

MISSING AUTOMATION: the file-level docstring claims 'Automatic File Lifecycle Management' and 'Called on every session start' (lines 5, 23) but nothing inside st8 actually invokes runBrunoCall or runOscarHouse on a schedule. They only fire via POST /api/bruno-call and POST /api/oscar-house. Either remove the 'automatic' wording from the docstring OR wire a startup trigger (e.g. a SESSION_START hook subscriber that calls bruno) in src/core/server/main.js after persistence.initialize(). Today the feature is fully manual; the docstring is misleading.
actionsTaken: Wired bruno-oscar's runBrunoCall as an INDEX_START hook subscriber at priority 20 (after sonic-daemon P=10) in src/core/hooks/default-subscribers.js. main.js already fires HOOKS.INDEX_START via hookRegistry.execute(...) at boot (line 267) with ctx={targetDir, persistence}; the new subscriber lazy-requires BrunoOscar + notificationBus inside its body (same posture as the existing FILE_AFTER_CHANGE subscribers), constructs a BrunoOscar with the ctx.persistence handle, and awaits bruno.runBrunoCall() with the default STALE_THRESHOLD=5. Side effect: every backend boot now performs the active->flagged sweep automatically; flagged-count is logged when > 0. The docstring claim 'Called on every session start' is now true rather than aspirational. Updated the runBrunoCall jsdoc to point at the subscriber site as the canonical caller. Updated the pre-existing hook-registry.test.js assertions to expect 2 INDEX_START subscribers (was 1) - sonic-daemon (P=10) + bruno-session-start (P=20). New tests in tests/features/lifecycle/bruno-oscar.test.js: (a) static-shape probe asserts bruno-session-start is registered on INDEX_START AND runs AFTER sonic-daemon via introspectExecuteOrder, (b) hermetic effect probe builds a fresh HookRegistry, registers ONLY the bruno-session-start handler body (verbatim copy, avoids sonic-daemon spawn side effect inside the unit-test process), fires INDEX_START with real ctx, asserts both stale rows in file_registry flipped to brunoStatus='flagged' while the fresh row stayed 'active'. Full suite 223->230 (+7 across all 3 wave-4B subscriber/publisher tickets).
residualConcerns: Two residuals, neither blocking: (1) runOscarHouse is NOT wired as a subscriber - Wave 4B scope is bruno (session-start flag sweep), not oscar (archive sweep). Today oscar still only fires via POST /api/oscar-house. Roadmap Priority 2 (Bruno+Oscar state-machine) is the right home for an automatic oscar trigger. (2) Pre-existing upsertFile bug surfaced by live boot probe: bruno fires at INDEX_START (P=20) and correctly flips brunoStatus='flagged' for stale rows; the indexer's subsequent Pass-1 upsert loop (main.js:288-318) calls persistence.upsertFile which does INSERT OR REPLACE WITHOUT brunoStatus in the column list - SQLite then defaults brunoStatus back to 'active' on every replace. Net effect: bruno's flag is visible during INDEX_START but reset before INDEX_COMPLETE writes the manifest. The bug is in upsertFile (persistence cluster), not the subscriber wiring - it predates Wave 4B and was not introduced by this wave. Live boot probe evidence: '[bruno] Flagged 1 stale files for review' AND '[st8] Bruno session-start scan: flagged 1 stale file(s)' both printed before the upsert pass overwrote the row. Recommend follow-up persistence ticket to make upsertFile preserve brunoStatus + sessionsSinceAccess + lastAccessed across replaces.
reviewNote: Verified default-subscribers.js:114-127 registers bruno-session-start at priority=20 on HOOKS.INDEX_START. Lazy require + try/catch wraps the body per the DRY+wrap convention; failure does not poison the chain. tests/core/hook-registry.test.js correctly updated to expect 2 INDEX_START subscribers (sonic-daemon P=10 + bruno-session-start P=20). The shape probe and hermetic-effect probe in bruno-oscar.test.js cover both registration order and per-row brunoStatus side effect. upsertFile clobber confirmed independently (persistence.js:500-522 omits brunoStatus from INSERT OR REPLACE column list) — documented as cross-cluster pre-existing bug in review.md, NOT a kickback for this ticket.

userNote: DEAD HOOK SITE: this is the natural firing site for HOOKS.LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION (per batch 025 narrative: 'LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION stays reserved for actual file-phase transitions — bruno+oscar territory'). runOscarHouse changes brunoStatus from 'flagged' to 'archived' and never calls hookRegistry.execute. onEventTriggered changes it back to 'active' and never fires the hook either. Wire HOOKS.LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION with payload { file, oldPhase: 'flagged', newPhase: 'archived' } in runOscarHouse, and the inverse in onEventTriggered. The hook constant exists; the publisher is missing.
actionsTaken: Added a _fireLifecycleTransition(file, oldPhase, newPhase) helper on BrunoOscar that lazy-requires hook-registry (avoids constructor-time circular concerns and lets tests construct BrunoOscar without the registry singleton). Wired three publisher sites with the canonical {file:{fingerprint,filepath}, oldPhase, newPhase} payload: (1) runBrunoCall fires active->flagged per flagged file, (2) runOscarHouse fires flagged->archived per archived file, (3) onEventTriggered fires archived->active per un-archived file. The three methods are now async (await this._fireLifecycleTransition awaits subscribers in priority order per hook-registry contract). Updated POST /api/bruno-call and POST /api/oscar-house in src/core/server/app.js to await the now-async results before responding (`async () => { ... const result = await bruno.runBrunoCall(threshold); ... }`). New tests in tests/features/lifecycle/bruno-oscar.test.js register a REAL subscriber on the singleton hookRegistry (the lazy-require path bruno-oscar actually uses), trigger each of the three transition methods, and assert: (a) per-stale-file payload on active->flagged, (b) per-archived-file payload on flagged->archived, (c) per-event-match payload on archived->active. Each test unsubscribes its capture in a finally block so the singleton stays clean for the rest of the suite. Subscriber receives correct {file.fingerprint, file.filepath, oldPhase, newPhase}.
residualConcerns: Hook payload semantics: the canonical hook-registry.js docstring for LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION says 'lifecyclePhase' transitions, but bruno-oscar's transitions are brunoStatus (active/flagged/archived), not lifecyclePhase (DEVELOPMENT/PRODUCTION/etc). Ticket 10 instructed exactly this wiring ('Wire HOOKS.LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION with payload { file, oldPhase: 'flagged', newPhase: 'archived' }'), and the hook header comment in hook-registry.js explicitly says 'bruno+oscar archive/unarchive (today only updates brunoStatus, not lifecyclePhase ... when oscar gains real phase transitions it should fire here)' - so the wiring follows the documented intent. A future roadmap item could split the hook into two (LIFECYCLE_PHASE_TRANSITION vs BRUNO_STATUS_TRANSITION) if the conflation becomes a problem; today subscribers can disambiguate on oldPhase/newPhase values.
reviewNote: Three publisher sites confirmed: runBrunoCall:87 (active->flagged), runOscarHouse:148 (flagged->archived), onEventTriggered:215 (archived->active). _fireLifecycleTransition helper (lines 40-53) lazy-requires hook-registry and try/catches around hookRegistry.execute so a registry fault never blocks the primary brunoStatus mutation. app.js POST /api/bruno-call and POST /api/oscar-house await the now-async results (commit 7c4b673). Mutation probe: commented out the active->flagged fire — exactly the matching test failed (`not ok 1 - runBrunoCall fires LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION active→flagged per stale file`). Tests register a REAL subscriber on the singleton hookRegistry and unsubscribe in finally — singleton stays clean. Hook-semantics residual (brunoStatus reusing LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION) is documented and acceptable for now.

userNote: DEAD CODE / UNWIRED: _appendToParent (lines 162-182) reads a file, appends its content to the file referenced by associatedWith, and is declared private. Grep across src/ shows zero call sites — runOscarHouse never invokes it. The 'fold stale child into parent before archive' behavior described in the comment is implemented but not connected. Either wire it into runOscarHouse for files with associatedWith set, or remove the method.
actionsTaken: Decision: WIRE-UP, not delete. _appendToParent is documented in bruno-oscar.js's per-method jsdoc as 'Called when archiving a file that has an associated parent' - the intent is preserved code, the wiring was missing. Added `this._appendToParent(file);` at the top of the per-file try-block inside runOscarHouse, BEFORE the archiveFile call so the parent receives the child's content before the brunoStatus flip. _appendToParent is itself defensive: returns false silently when file.associatedWith is unset (the common case - most flagged files have no parent), AND wraps the readFileSync/appendFileSync in try/catch so a missing parent or read error logs '[oscar] Failed to append ...' and returns false rather than aborting the archive. The archive proceeds regardless of the fold result - the fold is a courtesy, not a prerequisite. Test in tests/features/lifecycle/bruno-oscar.test.js: (a) flagged file with associatedWith pointing at an existing parent.md on disk - after runOscarHouse, parent.md contains the appended child content marked with 'APPENDED BY OSCAR' marker, (b) flagged file with associatedWith=null - runOscarHouse completes cleanly, brunoStatus flips to 'archived', no file system fault.
residualConcerns: _appendToParent reads file.filepath directly with fs.readFileSync - bruno-oscar treats this as a resolvable path. The test writes both parent and child to a temp dir and uses absolute paths in file_registry, which matches how main.js's watcher records filepaths today (relative-to-targetDir). If a future caller passes a non-resolvable filepath, the inner try/catch logs and returns false - acceptable degraded behavior.
reviewNote: Verified _appendToParent called at bruno-oscar.js:133 inside runOscarHouse's per-file try-block BEFORE archiveFile. Best-effort posture: returns false on missing associatedWith / non-existent parent / read error, wrapped in inner try/catch so it cannot abort the archive. Tests cover both branches: parent appended with the 'APPENDED BY OSCAR' marker when associatedWith is set on disk, and clean archive with no FS fault when associatedWith=null. Call path traced through runOscarHouse — not test-only invocation.

userNote: SINGLE-TARGET LIMIT: FileWatcher is constructed once at line 209 with the single targetDir from argv. Multi-target setups (e.g. monorepos where the frontend and a sibling backend repo both want indexing) are unsupported. The FileWatcher class itself accepts only one root in its constructor. To extend: either (a) accept multiple --watch args and spin up N watchers, or (b) extend FileWatcher to accept a roots array and configure chokidar with the array form chokidar.watch([...]). Document the limitation in the meantime.
actionsTaken: DEFERRED to roadmap. Multi-target watcher is roadmap-scale work: (a) FileWatcher constructor must accept roots[] and pass the array form to chokidar.watch(); (b) main.js argv parser must accept repeated --watch <dir> or a comma-separated list; (c) manifest writer (writeManifests + connection-state.json shape) needs per-root keyed output OR a flattened single-manifest with root-prefixed filepaths; (d) the shared St8Persistence handle must partition file_registry rows by root (today filepath is relative-to-single-targetDir — multi-root would collide); (e) /api/mutations SSE channel needs root tagging so multi-root subscribers can filter. The existing roadmap entry at docs/_pending-roadmap/lifecycle-and-eventing.md (Priority 3: 'Multiple-target-dir support in the watcher') already captures the design surface; this defer adds nothing structurally new. No source change in Wave 4B — the work is correctly sized as its own future batch, not a sub-wave extra.
residualConcerns: Roadmap pointer: docs/_pending-roadmap/lifecycle-and-eventing.md — 'Priority 3 — Multiple-target-dir support in the watcher'. Single-target remains the supported mode.
reviewNote: Defer is correct. Roadmap entry already exists at docs/_pending-roadmap/lifecycle-and-eventing.md line 22 ('Priority 3 — Multiple-target-dir support in the watcher') and predates this ticket. The executor's design-surface enumeration (FileWatcher roots[], argv parser, per-root manifest keying, file_registry partition, SSE root-tagging) accurately scopes the work as a roadmap batch, not a sub-wave extra. No source change in Wave 4B — correct posture.

userNote: BROAD IGNORES: the ignored-globs list at lines 55-72 includes '**/*.json' and '**/*.toml'. This is to prevent the manifest writer's own output (connection-state.json, ai-signal.toml) from triggering the watcher, but it also ignores user code config files (package.json, tsconfig.json, pyproject.toml). Tighten to specific basenames (connection-state.json, ai-signal.toml) plus the .st8/** glob already present, OR rely solely on the indexer's batch-026 SELF_WRITTEN_BASENAMES guard. Today a package.json change does not trigger re-index.
actionsTaken: Audit verdict: scope DOWN. The broad `**/*.json` + `**/*.toml` ignores were over-scoped after Wave 2B + batch 026: (a) the indexer already has SELF_WRITTEN_BASENAMES = {'connection-state.json', 'ai-signal.toml'} at src/features/indexing/indexer.js:166 that skips those basenames at index time, (b) main.js's CODE_EXTENSIONS filter still includes `.json` (so JSON files are legitimate code targets), (c) the indexer's IGNORE_DIRS already covers .st8/ which holds .json manifests. Replaced `**/*.json` + `**/*.toml` with `path.join(this.targetDir, 'connection-state.json')` + `path.join(this.targetDir, 'ai-signal.toml')` — root-only, exact-path. Now a user `package.json` / `tsconfig.json` / `pyproject.toml` / `Cargo.toml` change correctly flows through the watcher. The other ignores (.sqlite*, node_modules, .git, dist, build, .venv, venv, __pycache__, .st8/**, snapshots/**, .archive/**, .planning/st8_identity_system/**) were left intact — they are scoped to known non-source paths. Inline comment block added above the ignored: list explaining the audit decision and the downstream safety net (CODE_EXTENSIONS + SELF_WRITTEN_BASENAMES).
reviewNote: Verified `**/*.json` and `**/*.toml` removed; replaced by absolute root-only paths `path.join(targetDir,'connection-state.json')` and `path.join(targetDir,'ai-signal.toml')`. node_modules/.git/.st8/dist/build/__pycache__/.venv/venv/snapshots/.archive/.sqlite globs all intact — no accidental re-enabling of node_modules/**/*.json or .git/**. Inline comment block documents the audit decision plus downstream safety nets (CODE_EXTENSIONS + SELF_WRITTEN_BASENAMES). Subdir-manifest residual is bounded and acceptable.

userNote: NO METRICS: the watcher has no observability — no count of events received, no debounce-merge ratio (input events / flush calls), no last-flush latency. For a long-running --watch session this is the first signal that something is wrong (chokidar event storm, debounce too short, callback blocking too long). Add a lightweight stats object: { eventsReceived, flushes, lastFlushDuration, lastFlushChanges } exposed via a GET /api/watcher/stats endpoint OR logged every N flushes.
actionsTaken: Added a `_metrics` counter object initialised in the constructor with five fields: eventsReceived (every event reaching _onFileChange post-ignore-list), debounceMergeCount (events that collapsed onto an existing pendingChanges key — proves the ticket-4 dedup is firing), flushCalls (number of _flush() invocations), lastFlushAt (ISO timestamp of the most recent flush; null pre-first-flush), lastFlushSize (size of the change array at the most recent flush). _onFileChange increments eventsReceived and conditionally debounceMergeCount before set(). _flush() increments flushCalls + sets lastFlushAt + lastFlushSize. Exposed via a new public getMetrics() method that returns a shallow snapshot ({ ..._metrics }) so callers cannot mutate internal state. Smoke probe: 3 events on 2 distinct (path,type) keys, debounceMergeCount==1, post-flush flushCalls==1, lastFlushSize==2, lastFlushAt is an ISO string. No /api wiring per ticket guidance (frontend cluster's concern). Test coverage in ticket 3.
reviewNote: Verified _metrics initialised in constructor with the 5 required counters (eventsReceived, debounceMergeCount, flushCalls, lastFlushAt:null, lastFlushSize:0). getMetrics() returns `{ ..._metrics }` shallow copy. Out-of-band isolation probe: `a.eventsReceived = 99999; b = getMetrics()` → mutated a=99999, fresh b=0. Internal state untouched. Counters increment at the right call sites (eventsReceived pre-check, debounceMergeCount on has(key), flushCalls/lastFlushAt/lastFlushSize in _flush). Shallow copy is safe for the current flat-primitive shape.

userNote: STALE COMMENT: header comment (lines 1-10) describes 'multi-LLM manifest synchronization' as the use case — two LLMs reading the same manifest, watcher fires, manifest updates, second LLM sees truth. The actual call site (src/frontend/app.js:363) just starts polling with no addListener consumer anywhere in src/frontend/. Either the multi-LLM design has been quietly deprecated (in which case retire to OGB per the batch-025 state.js precedent), or there's a missing call site to surface listener output. Reconcile the comment with reality.

DORMANT PRINTER CHAIN: setPrinter is called from main.js:92, but the publish() path only invokes printer.printCard when the event carries a schemaCard property — no current publisher attaches that property. main.js emits schema cards out-of-band via emitter.emitCard. Either wire the watcher branches to attach the card (single point of card emission) OR remove the printer hook from publish() and call printer.printCard directly from the emitter path. Today the conditional is structurally dead.
actionsTaken: Audit verdict: outcome (c) — accidentally dead, now wired correctly. setPrinter(printer) is called in src/core/server/main.js:252; notification-bus.publish() guards on `if (this.printer && event.schemaCard)`. Before this ticket, NO publisher attached a schemaCard property to the event — the FILE_AFTER_CHANGE P=30 SSE-broadcaster subscriber published with only fingerprint/filepath/mutationType/actor/sha256Hash. The schema-card-emitter P=20 subscriber DOES emit the card but threw the return value away, so the printer chain was structurally dormant. Wire-up: (1) Captured emitter.emitCard()'s return value into ctx.schemaCard inside the P=20 subscriber, (2) P=30 subscriber now publishes `schemaCard: ctx.schemaCard || null` so the printer's printCard guard fires on every watcher CREATE/EDIT (DELETE path is unchanged — the P=20 subscriber returns early without emitting for unlinks, ctx.schemaCard stays null, printer guard skips). Test: tests/core/hooks/file-after-change-printer-wire.test.js fires the REAL FILE_AFTER_CHANGE chain (real HookRegistry + real registerDefaultSubscribers + real SchemaCardEmitter + real notificationBus singleton with a stub printer) and asserts (a) EDIT path invokes printer.printCard once with the correct fingerprint+filepath, (b) DELETE path does NOT invoke the printer. Mutation probe confirmed: replacing `ctx.schemaCard = emittedCard;` with a no-op flipped the EDIT test from pass to fail (got 0 calls instead of 1) — exactly the target failure. Also added 4 unit-level probes in tests/core/notification-bus.test.js covering printCard fires/skips on schemaCard present/absent/null and the throw-catch isolation (printer crash does not abort the EventEmitter step that ran before it). INDEX_COMPLETE printer path (printAllFromCards via P=20 subscriber in default-subscribers.js:157) was already wired separately at batch-time and remains untouched — this ticket addresses ONLY the per-file watcher notification path.
residualConcerns: The printer.printCard runs synchronously inside publish() — a slow filesystem (.txt write to .planning/st8_identity_system/) will block the publish loop for a few ms per change. Acceptable today (single-target, single-watcher), but if Multi-target lands (deferred ticket 12) the cumulative latency on a large flush warrants a queue. Out of scope for Wave 4C.
